Sabertooth Amulet  
 

Lore Item
Slot: NECK
AC: 4
STA: +5 WIS: +5
SV MAGIC: +15
Effect: See Invisible (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 0.4 Size: TINY
Class: SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Sabertooth Amulet
Lore Item
Slot: NECK
AC: 4
STA: +5 WIS: +5
SV MAGIC: +15
Effect: See Invisible (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 0.4 Size: TINY
Class: SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Jewelry
Stackable:No

Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark

Rarity: Rare

▲ Page top
Great item
QuoteReply
# Jul 04 2002 at 3:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778774587
2 posts
A good friend of mine just gave this to me as a gift yesterday. Although the Int instead of Wis puzzles me (how silly), I'm enjoying the permanent See Invis and the MR. Considering that I only had a +2 Wis item on my neck before, talk about an improvement!

Fjall Aefinlegur
38 Shaman Ayonae Ro
▲ Page top
Umm...
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2002 at 9:13 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776410526
Sage
280 posts
I will be heading to TT to get OS key shortly and came here to check out what mobs/loot is in this zone. Any loot is good, but this isnt very usefull. The AC is too low and WTF is Verant thinking putting INT on a shaman only item? Its because of stupid crap like this that I cant get my shaman to 200 WIS or better. See invis is nice especially since it doesnt take up a spell or buff slot, but if I get one of these it will be for sale in EC to some shaman who thinks its "cool". As Beavis and Butthead would say " Uh huh huh umm... this sucks".

54 Ogre Battlemystic on Luclin server--Aggrok
▲ Page top
Awsome for Shaman
QuoteReply
# Dec 09 2001 at 10:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768349762
View User Forum
Scholar
55 posts
I would have to say this is a awsome neck item for shaman. See invis permanent. The most important fact is the 25 svmagic. I have gone over 200 wis a long time ago and the item was easy to fit into my neck slot. I was doing the Seb keys for guildmates when this thing dropped. he was a easy kill and should be killed when a group is doing the seb key cycle. Any 50+ shammy in the guild should love to have this item given to them =)

Lelandpain Eversow
53 Iksar Shaman
<Exile>
Brell
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2003 at 3:18 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766929078
132 posts
Its only +15 magic resitance mate=)
▲ Page top
My new Shaman
QuoteReply
# Oct 08 2001 at 1:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766140524
11 posts
I am confused (blonde moment), I thought shaman were wisdom casters? I understand the see invisible is great and all but why make an item that increases a stat that I do not particularly use? Just a wandering mind speaking drivle =)
▲ Page top
RE: My new Shaman
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 9:46 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965864169
76 posts
Verant doesn't seem to pay a tremendous amount of attention to the "important" stats for any given class. Look at the 30-ish cleric quest armor (only quest armor for the 30's that I'm really familiar with as my cleric quested the whole set and is still wearing some). Let's see:
Greaves of the Penitent +13 AC +5 Int +5 vsPos +5.60 Wt
Chestplate of the Constant +20 AC +8 Sta +5 vsMg +7.50 Wt
Gauntlets of the Ardent +12 AC +5 Agi +5 vsCold +3.80 Wt
Boots of the Reliant +11 AC +6 vsDis +4.90 Wt
Vambraces of the Fervent +11 AC +5 Dex +5 vsFire +4.90 Wt
Bracers of the Reverent +9 AC +5 Wis +3.00 Wt (very nice!)

Only one item has +Wis (the bracers which fortunately are not lore, and so you can wear two for +10 wis 8D) The other stats are moderately useful (I don't care what other people say, modifying my dex by 40-some points hasn't seemed to affect my ability to regain concentration) +sta +int +agi are pretty useless too (ok, agi helps some, and sta gives you a few hitpoints, but gimme some mana!)
▲ Page top
Silvermane was a Pussy...cat :)
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2001 at 9:54 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591761453921
Scholar
45 posts
Got this item today when I literally bumped into Silvermane in Trak's Teeth. He was a level 50 mob (at least in this spawn) but was not that tough to take down. He didn't resist any spells and it was pretty much a straight-forward root and DoT scenario. This was the only loot he had on him...
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 15 2001 at 12:37 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i am sellin gone of these on the fennin ro server i wil sell for a cheapoer price just so i can get it out of my inventory i wil sell for around 2k or less just offer me or a good trade would be nice
great item
QuoteReply
# Jul 03 2001 at 12:27 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757775621
4 posts
the see invisible effect is permanent not triggered.
▲ Page top
Nice Piece, regardless of the int instead of wis
QuoteReply
# Jun 27 2001 at 9:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245941594
Scholar
93 posts
I have this piece, and wear it often, depending on what I'm doing at any given time. The see invis is very nice to have in places like Velkator's Labrynth, where some of the mobs are actually invis, and the magic resist is a must in most higher lvl dungeons. Well worth the swap in wis in many instances, IMHO.
▲ Page top
nice !
QuoteReply
# May 18 2001 at 12:56 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
the effect isn't triggered - tis permanent .. superb neck piece if you ask me ;o)
▲ Page top
int is good for large races
QuoteReply
# Apr 14 2001 at 8:58 AM Rating: Decent
ArtulynStarsummoner
Sage
179 posts
I think the idea behind this item is that by the time you get it you can swap out some wis gear for other stats. Considering the fact that all of the large races start with very low int this would be a great item for finally getting your skills to max out. Especially if your trying to develop a tradeskill for one of those multi-upgrade velious quests.
____________________________
Artulyn Starsummoner 120 Grand Summoner of Omani Winter on Bertoxxulous

For the Light of a Candle to be Seen, It must be taken to a Dark Place -- UKLG

RE: int is good for large races
QuoteReply
# Jun 10 2001 at 1:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361136062
26 posts
Its Stated That Whichever Stats is higher, ie. Wis or Int, is your primary increase for trades.
so since all shamen have there wis higher than there int this items is even useless for trades considering the int, althou the other stats are phat
▲ Page top
Hardly Useless
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2001 at 9:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750508920
View User Forum
8 posts
3 of the 4 bonuses on this are very good. The +INT bonus has me a little confused, but +15 MR makes this item a hugely useful one, once you get into your mid-40s and beyond.

useless?
QuoteReply
# Jan 22 2001 at 1:17 AM Rating: Default

Anonymous
Anyone know anything about this item? it is useless as far as i can see....

RE: useless?
QuoteReply
# May 18 2001 at 8:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1696205787970
Sage
190 posts
Are you MAD?

You have a 'See Invis' effect which, either triggered or perm, is good. Add those throw-in INT and STA mods followed by that +15MagResist to the equation and you have one NICE neck piece.

I hope you were being sarcastic...that's the only thing i can think of to make someone say this is useless....I only wish Rangers could use it! It would make sense to trade the INT for WIS but when has Verant ever made sense? :)
